text: Dear students, Welcome to Gate Smashers In today's video I am going to explain MapReduce which is one of the core component of Apache Hadoop As I told you in the last video about HDFS What is the purpose of HDFS That my big data, large amount of data Instead of storing it in one place I am storing it in a distributed way Means I am partitioning it in different places and storing it So when my processing unit will come Processing unit means that data is stored Now what to do after storing it We have to extract some important information or insights from it So who will extract it? The processing unit will extract it Means the one who will process this data So the processing unit should also be such Which can process this whole data in a distributed way So for that purpose, MapReduce was specially designed So what is MapReduce? MapReduce performs the processing of large amount of data set In a distributed and parallel manner Data is already distributed Data is already stored in a distributed way by HDFS Now I need a system which can process this data in parallel So that my time is less and I get more efficiency So that is the purpose of MapReduce So we call it as Processing Unit of Apache Hadoop So see, in MapReduce, you can say two main classes You can say two main tasks One is Map and the other is Reduce We generally say that there is one class that is Mapper class And another class that is Reduce Because this works on divide and conquer approach Maybe if you have heard in the algorithm What is divide and conquer approach? That I have a very big problem First you divide it in small partitions, small portions Keep dividing it, keep dividing it Till it comes on a very small level When it is split, divided Finally, what we do is process it and then reduce it Reduce means combine the intermediate results and generate the final output So what is the work of Mapper? To solve the big problem in small problems And then convert them into key value pairs Means what is the key and what is its value? So it converts the data into key value pair after splitting Then after that, it is processed Whatever we write, whatever program we write here So based on that program, whatever work I have to do, that work will be performed But it will be parallel to all the split we have done on all the programs And then the intermediate result will come What will we do to that result? By combining, which is called reducer class By reducing the reducer, the intermediate result converts it into the final result I will explain this to you with real life example here, don't worry Then another important point comes here That two main daemons come here who work on the background What are daemons? Those processes that work on the background So there we have a job tracker and a task tracker So what is the job tracker? It is the boss Here the job tracker is the boss Whose work is to schedule the process That which job will be scheduled, where will it be scheduled Provide resources Means this is the operating system kind of This is the boss who manages all the things And the one who actually works is your task tracker Like we did in HDFS, this is the name node So what is the purpose of the name node? It is a kind of boss who stores all the metadata Who tells which data to be stored where But where is the data stored? In the data node I am telling you the same thing here That you will be clear from my diagram What is the job tracker here? It works like a master node Works like a boss Like HDFS, what is this HDFS component? What is this? Map reduce component So here this is the master You can say this is your slave node Whose actual work is to do This is the employee Like this is the employee of the company who is actually working And this is the boss, the master who is managing all the things Like in HDFS, the name node and data node are the same thing here So what is its job? Its job is to schedule things That which thing will be scheduled at what time Provide resources Provide resources The resources I need The resources that the task tracker needs It provides Monitoring along with it Means things are running properly or not Any fault has occurred Any task tracker is not working So all these things are managed All this is your job tracker And then the task tracker actually works Where the data is lying Now here is a very important point That we say that how do we parallel access such a large amount of data? So a very important concept comes here What usually happens is that my data We have kept it on different data nodes here in this way What do we do here?
